title: Roundoff — when 0.1 + 0.2 isn't 0.3
problem: Add 0.1 + 0.2 and check whether the result equals 0.3.

trace.note: total is 0.1 + 0.2 — but look closely: it is 0.30000000000000004, not 0.3. Neither 0.1 nor 0.2 has an exact binary fraction, so the sum lands a hair off.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: We added 0.1 + 0.2. After `is_exact = total == 0.3` runs, will is_exact be True or False? (Add a variable named is_exact and predict it.)

trace.note: is_exact = total == 0.3 is False. That tiny tail means total is NOT exactly 0.3, so == fails — even though the maths looks right on paper.

result = 'off by a hair'. Comparing floats with == is a classic trap. This is ROUNDOFF — a representation error from squeezing decimals into binary — and it is completely different from overflow, which is a fixed-width wrap. CPython shows the gap; we don't have to claim it.
